title: IPv6 proxy URL passes bracketed host to ToProxyAddrs
description:
parse_proxy_addr('socks5h://[::1]:9050') returns proxy_host='[::1]' with brackets intact.

The bracketed string is then passed to Socks5Stream::connect as the first argument (proxy address). tokio-socks uses ToProxyAddrs for (&str, u16), which delegates to std::net::ToSocketAddrs via to_socket_addrs(). The behavior of ('[::1]', 9050).to_socket_addrs() is unspecified: on Linux glibc it may treat '[::1]' as a DNS hostname (DNS lookup fails) rather than an IPv6 literal, producing a confusing resolution error instead of a connection attempt.

This is the mirror of the relay-side bracket bug. The fix is the same: strip brackets from any bracketed IPv6 host string returned by parse_proxy_addr before use.
